channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Wildfire damages 20 properties near Clinton, B.C.
item.description: A wildfire burning northwest of Clinton, B.C., has damaged or destroyed buildings on about 20 properties, according to regional district director Kevin Skrepnek. The fire, which spans over 108 square ...

item.title: Nigeria's Army Expands to 12 Divisions with 28,000 New Recruits
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has approved the expansion of the Nigerian Army from 8 to 12 divisions, alongside the recruitment of 28,000 additional personnel, to strengthen national security. The implementat...

item.title: Philippines Asserts Sovereignty Over Ayungin Shoal, Rejects China's Claims
item.description: The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) reiterated that the Philippines holds sovereign rights over Ayungin Shoal, stating there is no need for permission to be present in the disputed area. This come...

item.title: Crikey investigates AI lawsuit settlement and media ethics disclosure
item.description: The article discusses Crikey's coverage of a class action lawsuit settlement involving AI giant Anthropic, where the company agreed to pay over A$2 billion to thousands of authors. It highlights Crike...

item.title: Raila Junior Embraces Simplicity with Githeri Breakfast at Uncle's Home
item.description: Raila Junior, son of prominent Kenyan politician Raila Odinga, shared a video of a casual breakfast with his uncle Oburu Oginga at his home. The meal featured githeri, a traditional mix of maize and b...

item.title: Obasanjo refutes Osoba's claims over 2003 election politics
item.description: Former President Olusegun Obasanjo has strongly denied allegations by ex-Ogun Governor Olusegun Osoba that he deceived AD governors and Afenifere leaders ahead of the 2003 elections. In a detailed let...

item.title: US launches military strikes on Iran amid Houthi attacks on Saudi tankers, impacting global oil prices
item.description: The US has intensified military strikes on Iran for the 13th consecutive day following Houthi attacks on Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea. The conflict, which began in late February, has escalated ten...

item.title: Ethiopia's East Arsi Region Faces Escalating Violence and Calls for Immediate Action
item.description: The article details a series of armed attacks targeting Orthodox Christian communities in Ethiopia's Oromia Regional State, specifically in the East Arsi Zone. Over 53 Ethiopian Orthodox Tewahedo Chri...

item.title: Mohamed Ali Comments on Albert Ojwang Murder Case Amid New CCTV Evidence
item.description: MP Mohamed Ali, known as Jicho Pevu, has spoken out about the ongoing murder trial of Albert Ojwang, highlighting new CCTV evidence presented in court. The High Court in Kibera adjourned the case to D...

item.title: Ted Lasso Season 4: New Direction with Returning Cast and Female Team Focus
item.description: Season four of Apple TV’s Ted Lasso, starring Jason Sudeikis as the titular coach, is set to premiere on August 5. The series, which previously followed the fictional AFC Richmond men’s soccer team, i...

item.title: Burnham's First Day at No 10 North: Devolution Plans and Political Criticism
item.description: Prime Minister Andy Burnham will hold his first meeting at the new No 10 North in Manchester, marking a shift in power from central London to regional hubs. The initiative aims to decentralize governa...

item.title: Backlash Against Trump's Saudi Nuclear Deal Sparks Debate on National Security
item.description: White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt defended the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal, arguing it benefits American companies and workers. However, her comments drew significant criticism from political c...

item.title: India's Construction Sector Faces Environmental Challenges with Rising Carbon Emissions
item.description: The article highlights how India's rapid urbanization and construction boom are creating significant carbon emissions, which are often overlooked in environmental policies. It explains that the built ...

item.title: Johnny Depp Appears at Comic-Con in Role of Ebenezer Scrooge
item.description: Johnny Depp made a notable appearance at the San Diego Comic-Con, captivating fans by donning his latest character, Ebenezer Scrooge, as part of a theatrical performance. The event, held at a venue wi...

item.title: Wildfire in Fraser Canyon Produces Pyrocumulus Clouds Over British Columbia
item.description: Pyrocumulus clouds, formed by intense heat from wildfires, are developing above the Fraser Canyon region in British Columbia. These towering storm clouds, often associated with severe weather, indicat...

item.title: Upcoming Protests and Activities for Sona 2026 in the Philippines and Abroad
item.description: The article details the planned protests and activities related to the 2026 State of the Nation Address (SONA) in the Philippines and abroad. Organized by groups like BAYAN, Sulong UBC, and ALPAS Pili...

item.title: Government funds national hockey teams ahead of World Cup after financial concerns
item.description: Sport, arts and culture minister Gayton McKenzie has allocated R3 million to support the national men’s and women’s hockey teams for the upcoming FIH Hockey World Cup, co-hosted by Belgium and the Net...

item.title: Singida Black Stars Add Nigerian Defender to Squad
item.description: The Singida Black Stars, a prominent Tanzanian football team, have made a strategic move by signing Nigerian centre back Emmanuel Okoye to bolster their defensive line. This acquisition comes as the t...

item.title: Sky News Australia rebrands to News24 amid political and media shifts
item.description: Sky News Australia is rebranding to News24, marking a significant shift in its identity. The move comes as part of News Corp's strategy to retain the News24 brand name after its 10-year UK deal expire...

item.title: Parliament remains stalled for four consecutive days as Opposition demands Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation
item.description: The first four days of the Monsoon Session of Parliament have ended without any legislative business being conducted, signalling the possibility of a prolonged deadlock between the government and the ...
